tEn: XLNet: Generalized Autoregressive Pretraining for Language Understanding
tZh: XLNet：面向语言理解的广义自回归预训练
sEn: XLNet outperforms BERT on 20 tasks by learning bidirectional contexts via permutation language modeling and integrating Transformer-XL.
sZh: XLNet通过排列语言建模学习双向上下文并集成Transformer-XL，在20个任务上超越BERT。
absEn: With the capability of modeling bidirectional contexts, denoising autoencoding based pretraining like BERT achieves better performance than pretraining approaches based on autoregressive language modeling. However, relying on corrupting the input with masks, BERT neglects dependency between the masked positions and suffers from a pretrain-finetune discrepancy. In light of these pros and cons, we propose XLNet, a generalized autoregressive pretraining method that (1) enables learning bidirectional contexts by maximizing the expected likelihood over all permutations of the factorization order and (2) overcomes the limitations of BERT thanks to its autoregressive formulation. Furthermore, XLNet integrates ideas from Transformer-XL, the state-of-the-art autoregressive model, into pretraining. Empirically, under comparable experiment settings, XLNet outperforms BERT on 20 tasks, often by a large margin, including question answering, natural language inference, sentiment analysis, and document ranking.
absZh: 尽管基于去噪自编码的预训练方法（如BERT）通过建模双向上下文取得了比自回归语言模型更好的性能，但BERT依赖于对输入进行掩码破坏，忽略了掩码位置之间的依赖关系，并存在预训练与微调之间的差异。针对这些优缺点，我们提出了XLNet，一种广义自回归预训练方法，它通过最大化所有分解顺序排列的期望似然来学习双向上下文，并凭借其自回归公式克服了BERT的局限性。此外，XLNet将最先进的自回归模型Transformer-XL的思想整合到预训练中。实验表明，在可比较的实验设置下，XLNet在20个任务上（包括问答、自然语言推理、情感分析和文档排序）显著优于BERT，通常优势明显。

contrib.en: Proposes XLNet, a generalized autoregressive pretraining method that learns bidirectional contexts via permutation language modeling.
contrib.zh: 提出XLNet，一种通过排列语言建模学习双向上下文的广义自回归预训练方法。

contrib.en: Overcomes BERT's pretrain-finetune discrepancy by avoiding input corruption with masks.
contrib.zh: 通过避免使用掩码破坏输入，克服了BERT的预训练-微调差异。

contrib.en: Integrates Transformer-XL's segment recurrence and relative encoding for improved long-text handling.
contrib.zh: 集成Transformer-XL的段循环和相对编码，改进长文本处理。

contrib.en: Introduces two-stream self-attention to resolve target prediction ambiguity in permutation modeling.
contrib.zh: 引入双流自注意力机制，解决排列建模中的目标预测歧义。

contrib.en: Achieves state-of-the-art results on 20 NLP tasks, including QA, NLI, sentiment analysis, and document ranking.
contrib.zh: 在20个NLP任务上取得最先进结果，包括问答、自然语言推理、情感分析和文档排序。

contrib.en: Demonstrates that permutation LM and Transformer-XL both contribute to XLNet's superiority over BERT.
contrib.zh: 证明排列语言模型和Transformer-XL共同促成XLNet优于BERT。

limits.en: Permutation language modeling increases optimization difficulty, requiring partial prediction for convergence.
limits.zh: 排列语言建模增加优化难度，需要部分预测才能收敛。

limits.en: XLNet's performance gain is partly due to more training data and compute, not solely the objective.
limits.zh: XLNet的性能提升部分归因于更多训练数据和算力，而非仅目标函数。

limits.en: The two-stream attention mechanism adds architectural complexity and memory overhead.
limits.zh: 双流注意力机制增加了架构复杂性和内存开销。

limits.en: Ablation shows next-sentence prediction does not consistently improve XLNet, unlike BERT.
limits.zh: 消融实验表明，与BERT不同，下一句预测并未持续改进XLNet。

limits.en: XLNet's reliance on Transformer-XL may limit applicability to tasks with very short sequences.
limits.zh: XLNet对Transformer-XL的依赖可能限制其在极短序列任务上的适用性。

items.en: With the capability of modeling bidirectional contexts, denoising autoencoding based pretraining like BERT achieves better performance than pretraining approaches based on autoregressive language modeling. However, relying on corrupting the input with masks, BERT neglects dependency between the masked positions and suffers from a pretrain-finetune discrepancy. In light of these pros and cons, we propose XLNet, a generalized autoregressive pretraining method that (1) enables learning bidirectional contexts by maximizing the expected likelihood over all permutations of the factorization order and (2) overcomes the limitations of BERT thanks to its autoregressive formulation. Furthermore, XLNet integrates ideas from Transformer-XL, the state-of-the-art autoregressive model, into pretraining. Empirically, under comparable experiment settings, XLNet outperforms BERT on 20 tasks, often by a large margin, including question answering, natural language inference, sentiment analysis, and document ranking.111Pretrained models and code are available at https://github.com/zihangdai/xlnet.
items.zh: 凭借双向上下文建模能力，基于去噪自编码的预训练方法（如BERT）比基于自回归语言建模的预训练方法取得了更好的性能。然而，由于依赖掩码对输入进行破坏，BERT忽略了掩码位置之间的依赖性，并存在预训练-微调差异。鉴于这些优缺点，我们提出了XLNet，一种广义自回归预训练方法，它（1）通过最大化所有分解顺序排列的期望似然来学习双向上下文，并且（2）由于其自回归公式，克服了BERT的局限性。此外，XLNet将Transformer-XL（最先进的自回归模型）的思想整合到预训练中。实验表明，在可比较的实验设置下，XLNet在20个任务上（包括问答、自然语言推理、情感分析和文档排序）通常以较大优势优于BERT。预训练模型和代码可在https://github.com/zihangdai/xlnet获取。

items.en: Unsupervised representation learning has been highly successful in the domain of natural language processing [7, 22, 27, 28, 10]. Typically, these methods first pretrain neural networks on large-scale unlabeled text corpora, and then finetune the models or representations on downstream tasks. Under this shared high-level idea, different unsupervised pretraining objectives have been explored in literature. Among them, autoregressive (AR) language modeling and autoencoding (AE) have been the two most successful pretraining objectives.
items.zh: 无监督表示学习在自然语言处理领域取得了巨大成功[7, 22, 27, 28, 10]。通常，这些方法首先在大规模无标签文本语料库上预训练神经网络，然后在下游任务上微调模型或表示。在这种共享的高层思想下，文献中探索了不同的无监督预训练目标。其中，自回归（AR）语言建模和自编码（AE）是最成功的两种预训练目标。

items.en: AR language modeling seeks to estimate the probability distribution of a text corpus with an autoregressive model [7, 27, 28]. Specifically, given a text sequence 𝐱=(x1,⋯,xT)𝐱subscript𝑥1⋯subscript𝑥𝑇\mathbf{x}=(x_{1},\cdots,x_{T}), AR language modeling factorizes the likelihood into a forward product p​(𝐱)=∏t=1Tp​(xt∣𝐱<t)𝑝𝐱superscriptsubscriptproduct𝑡1𝑇𝑝conditionalsubscript𝑥𝑡subscript𝐱absent𝑡p(\mathbf{x})=\prod_{t=1}^{T}p(x_{t}\mid\mathbf{x}_{<t}) or a backward one p​(𝐱)=∏t=T1p​(xt∣𝐱>t)𝑝𝐱superscriptsubscriptproduct𝑡𝑇1𝑝conditionalsubscript𝑥𝑡subscript𝐱absent𝑡p(\mathbf{x})=\prod_{t=T}^{1}p(x_{t}\mid\mathbf{x}_{>t}). A parametric model (e.g. a neural network) is trained to model each conditional distribution. Since an AR language model is only trained to encode a uni-directional context (either forward or backward), it is not effective at modeling deep bidirectional contexts. On the contrary, downstream language understanding tasks often require bidirectional context information. This results in a gap between AR language modeling and effective pretraining.
items.zh: AR语言建模旨在通过自回归模型估计文本语料库的概率分布[7, 27, 28]。具体来说，给定文本序列𝐱=(x1,⋯,xT)，AR语言建模将似然分解为前向乘积p(𝐱)=∏t=1Tp(xt∣𝐱<t)或后向乘积p(𝐱)=∏t=T1p(xt∣𝐱>t)。训练参数化模型（如神经网络）来建模每个条件分布。由于AR语言模型仅训练编码单向上下文（前向或后向），它在建模深度双向上下文方面效果不佳。相反，下游语言理解任务通常需要双向上下文信息。这导致了AR语言建模与有效预训练之间的差距。

items.en: In comparison, AE based pretraining does not perform explicit density estimation but instead aims to reconstruct the original data from corrupted input. A notable example is BERT [10], which has been the state-of-the-art pretraining approach. Given the input token sequence, a certain portion of tokens are replaced by a special symbol [MASK], and the model is trained to recover the original tokens from the corrupted version. Since density estimation is not part of the objective, BERT is allowed to utilize bidirectional contexts for reconstruction. As an immediate benefit, this closes the aforementioned bidirectional information gap in AR language modeling, leading to improved performance. However, the artificial symbols like [MASK] used by BERT during pretraining are absent from real data at finetuning time, resulting in a pretrain-finetune discrepancy. Moreover, since the predicted tokens are masked in the input, BERT is not able to model the joint probability using the product rule as in AR language modeling. In other words, BERT assumes the predicted tokens are independent of each other given the unmasked tokens, which is oversimplified as high-order, long-range dependency is prevalent in natural language [9].
items.zh: 相比之下，基于AE的预训练不进行显式密度估计，而是旨在从损坏的输入中重建原始数据。一个显著的例子是BERT[10]，它一直是预训练方法中的最先进技术。给定输入词元序列，一定比例的词元被替换为特殊符号[MASK]，模型被训练从损坏版本中恢复原始词元。由于密度估计不是目标的一部分，BERT允许利用双向上下文进行重建。这直接的好处是弥补了AR语言建模中上述的双向信息差距，从而提高了性能。然而，BERT在预训练中使用的人工符号（如[MASK]）在微调时的真实数据中不存在，导致预训练-微调差异。此外，由于预测的词元在输入中被掩码，BERT无法像AR语言建模那样使用乘积规则建模联合概率。换句话说，BERT假设在给定未掩码词元的条件下，预测的词元相互独立，这过于简化，因为自然语言中普遍存在高阶、长距离依赖[9]。

items.en: Faced with the pros and cons of existing language pretraining objectives, in this work, we propose XLNet, a generalized autoregressive method that leverages the best of both AR language modeling and AE while avoiding their limitations.
items.zh: 面对现有语言预训练目标的优缺点，在这项工作中，我们提出了XLNet，一种广义自回归方法，它利用了AR语言建模和AE的最佳特性，同时避免了它们的局限性。

items.en: Firstly, instead of using a fixed forward or backward factorization order as in conventional AR models, XLNet maximizes the expected log likelihood of a sequence w.r.t. all possible permutations of the factorization order. Thanks to the permutation operation, the context for each position can consist of tokens from both left and right. In expectation, each position learns to utilize contextual information from all positions, i.e., capturing bidirectional context.
items.zh: 首先，XLNet不像传统AR模型那样使用固定的前向或后向分解顺序，而是最大化序列关于所有可能分解顺序排列的期望对数似然。由于排列操作，每个位置的上下文可以包含来自左右两侧的词元。在期望上，每个位置学会利用所有位置的上下文信息，即捕获双向上下文。

items.en: Secondly, as a generalized AR language model, XLNet does not rely on data corruption. Hence, XLNet does not suffer from the pretrain-finetune discrepancy that BERT is subject to. Meanwhile, the autoregressive objective also provides a natural way to use the product rule for factorizing the joint probability of the predicted tokens, eliminating the independence assumption made in BERT.
items.zh: 其次，作为广义AR语言模型，XLNet不依赖数据损坏。因此，XLNet不会遭受BERT所面临的预训练-微调差异。同时，自回归目标提供了一种自然的方式，使用乘积规则分解预测词元的联合概率，消除了BERT中做出的独立性假设。

items.en: In addition to a novel pretraining objective, XLNet improves architectural designs for pretraining.
items.zh: 除了新颖的预训练目标，XLNet还改进了预训练的架构设计。

items.en: Inspired by the latest advancements in AR language modeling, XLNet integrates the segment recurrence mechanism and relative encoding scheme of Transformer-XL [9] into pretraining, which empirically improves the performance especially for tasks involving a longer text sequence.
items.zh: 受AR语言建模最新进展的启发，XLNet将Transformer-XL[9]的片段循环机制和相对编码方案整合到预训练中，这实验性地提高了性能，特别是对于涉及较长文本序列的任务。

items.en: Naively applying a Transformer(-XL) architecture to permutation-based language modeling does not work because the factorization order is arbitrary and the target is ambiguous. As a solution, we propose to reparameterize the Transformer(-XL) network to remove the ambiguity.
items.zh: 天真地将Transformer(-XL)架构应用于基于排列的语言建模是行不通的，因为分解顺序是任意的，目标是不明确的。作为解决方案，我们提出重新参数化Transformer(-XL)网络以消除歧义。

items.en: Empirically, under comparable experiment setting, XLNet consistently outperforms BERT [10] on a wide spectrum of problems including GLUE language understanding tasks, reading comprehension tasks like SQuAD and RACE, text classification tasks such as Yelp and IMDB, and the ClueWeb09-B document ranking task.
items.zh: 实验表明，在可比较的实验设置下，XLNet在广泛的问题上持续优于BERT[10]，包括GLUE语言理解任务、阅读理解任务（如SQuAD和RACE）、文本分类任务（如Yelp和IMDB）以及ClueWeb09-B文档排序任务。

items.en: Related Work The idea of permutation-based AR modeling has been explored in [32, 12], but there are several key differences. Firstly, previous models aim to improve density estimation by baking an “orderless” inductive bias into the model while XLNet is motivated by enabling AR language models to learn bidirectional contexts. Technically, to construct a valid target-aware prediction distribution, XLNet incorporates the target position into the hidden state via two-stream attention while previous permutation-based AR models relied on implicit position awareness inherent to their MLP architectures. Finally, for both orderless NADE and XLNet, we would like to emphasize that “orderless” does not mean that the input sequence can be randomly permuted but that the model allows for different factorization orders of the distribution.
items.zh: 相关工作 基于排列的AR建模思想已在[32, 12]中探索过，但存在几个关键差异。首先，先前模型旨在通过向模型注入“无序”归纳偏差来改进密度估计，而XLNet的动机是使AR语言模型能够学习双向上下文。技术上，为了构建有效的目标感知预测分布，XLNet通过双流注意力将目标位置纳入隐藏状态，而先前的基于排列的AR模型依赖于其MLP架构固有的隐式位置感知。最后，对于无序NADE和XLNet，我们想强调“无序”并不意味着输入序列可以随机排列，而是模型允许分布的不同分解顺序。

items.en: Another related idea is to perform autoregressive denoising in the context of text generation [11], which only considers a fixed order though.
items.zh: 另一个相关思想是在文本生成上下文中执行自回归去噪[11]，尽管它只考虑固定顺序。

items.en: Input noise: The input to BERT contains artificial symbols like [MASK] that never occur in downstream tasks, which creates a pretrain-finetune discrepancy. Replacing [MASK] with original tokens as in [10] does not solve the problem because original tokens can be only used with a small probability — otherwise Eq. (2) will be trivial to optimize. In comparison, AR language modeling does not rely on any input corruption and does not suffer from this issue.
items.zh: 输入噪声：BERT的输入包含人工符号（如[MASK]），这些符号在下游任务中从未出现，这造成了预训练-微调差异。如[10]中那样用原始词元替换[MASK]并不能解决问题，因为原始词元只能以很小的概率使用——否则公式(2)的优化将变得平凡。相比之下，AR语言建模不依赖任何输入损坏，因此不存在此问题。

items.en: Context dependency: The AR representation hθ​(𝐱1:t−1)subscriptℎ𝜃subscript𝐱:1𝑡1h_{\theta}({\mathbf{x}}_{1:t-1}) is only conditioned on the tokens up to position t𝑡t (i.e. tokens to the left), while the BERT representation Hθ​(𝐱)tsubscript𝐻𝜃subscript𝐱𝑡H_{\theta}({\mathbf{x}})_{t} has access to the contextual information on both sides. As a result, the BERT objective allows the model to be pretrained to better capture bidirectional context.
items.zh: 上下文依赖性：AR表示hθ(𝐱1:t-1)仅以位置t之前的词元（即左侧词元）为条件，而BERT表示Hθ(𝐱)t可以访问两侧的上下文信息。因此，BERT目标允许模型预训练以更好地捕获双向上下文。

items.en: According to the comparison above, AR language modeling and BERT possess their unique advantages over the other. A natural question to ask is whether there exists a pretraining objective that brings the advantages of both while avoiding their weaknesses.
items.zh: 根据上述比较，AR语言建模和BERT各自拥有相对于对方的独特优势。一个自然的问题是，是否存在一种预训练目标，能够同时带来两者的优点并避免其缺点。

items.en: Borrowing ideas from orderless NADE [32], we propose the permutation language modeling objective that not only retains the benefits of AR models but also allows models to capture bidirectional contexts. Specifically, for a sequence 𝐱𝐱{\mathbf{x}} of length T𝑇T, there are T!𝑇T! different orders to perform a valid autoregressive factorization. Intuitively, if model parameters are shared across all factorization orders, in expectation, the model will learn to gather information from all positions on both sides.
items.zh: 借鉴无序NADE [32]的思想，我们提出了排列语言建模目标，它不仅保留了自回归模型的优势，还允许模型捕获双向上下文。具体来说，对于长度为T的序列𝐱，存在T!种不同的顺序来执行有效的自回归分解。直观上，如果模型参数在所有分解顺序之间共享，那么期望上，模型将学会从两侧的所有位置收集信息。

We use ztsubscript𝑧𝑡z_{t} and 𝐳<tsubscript𝐳absent𝑡{\mathbf{z}}_{<t} to denote the t𝑡t-th element and the first t−1𝑡1t-1 elements of a permutation 𝐳∈𝒵T𝐳subscript𝒵𝑇{\mathbf{z}}\in\mathcal{Z}_{T}. Then, our proposed permutation language modeling objective can be expressed as follows:
items.zh: 为了形式化这一思想，令𝒵_T为长度T的索引序列[1,2,…,T]的所有可能排列的集合。我们用z_t和𝐳_{<t}分别表示排列𝐳∈𝒵_T的第t个元素和前t-1个元素。那么，我们提出的排列语言建模目标可以表示如下：

items.en: Essentially, for a text sequence 𝐱𝐱{\mathbf{x}}, we sample a factorization order 𝐳𝐳{\mathbf{z}} at a time and decompose the likelihood pθ​(𝐱)subscript𝑝𝜃𝐱p_{\theta}({\mathbf{x}}) according to factorization order. Since the same model parameter θ𝜃\theta is shared across all factorization orders during training, in expectation, xtsubscript𝑥𝑡x_{t} has seen every possible element xi≠xtsubscript𝑥𝑖subscript𝑥𝑡x_{i}\neq x_{t} in the sequence, hence being able to capture the bidirectional context. Moreover, as this objective fits into the AR framework, it naturally avoids the independence assumption and the pretrain-finetune discrepancy discussed in Section 2.1.
items.zh: 本质上，对于文本序列𝐱，我们每次采样一个分解顺序𝐳，并根据分解顺序分解似然p_θ(𝐱)。由于训练期间所有分解顺序共享相同的模型参数θ，期望上，x_t已经看到了序列中所有可能的元素x_i≠x_t，因此能够捕获双向上下文。此外，由于该目标符合自回归框架，它自然避免了第2.1节讨论的独立性假设和预训练-微调差异。

items.en: Remark on Permutation The proposed objective only permutes the factorization order, not the sequence order. In other words, we keep the original sequence order, use the positional encodings corresponding to the original sequence, and rely on a proper attention mask in Transformers to achieve permutation of the factorization order. Note that this choice is necessary, since the model will only encounter text sequences with the natural order during finetuning.
items.zh: 关于排列的说明：所提出的目标仅排列分解顺序，而非序列顺序。换句话说，我们保持原始序列顺序，使用对应于原始序列的位置编码，并依赖Transformer中的适当注意力掩码来实现分解顺序的排列。注意，这一选择是必要的，因为模型在微调期间只会遇到自然顺序的文本序列。

items.en: To provide an overall picture, we show an example of predicting the token x3subscript𝑥3x_{3} given the same input sequence 𝐱𝐱{\mathbf{x}} but under different factorization orders in the Appendix A.7 with Figure 4.

items.en: While the permutation language modeling objective has desired properties, naive implementation with standard Transformer parameterization may not work. To see the problem, assume we parameterize the next-token distribution pθ​(Xzt∣𝐱𝐳<t)subscript𝑝𝜃conditionalsubscript𝑋subscript𝑧𝑡subscript𝐱subscript𝐳absent𝑡p_{\theta}(X_{z_{t}}\mid{\mathbf{x}}_{{\mathbf{z}}_{<t}}) using the standard Softmax formulation, i.e., pθ​(Xzt=x∣𝐱𝐳<t)=exp⁡(e​(x)⊤​hθ​(𝐱𝐳<t))∑x′exp⁡(e​(x′)⊤​hθ​(𝐱𝐳<t)),subscript𝑝𝜃subscript𝑋subscript𝑧𝑡conditional𝑥subscript𝐱subscript𝐳absent𝑡𝑒superscript𝑥topsubscriptℎ𝜃subscript𝐱subscript𝐳absent𝑡subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑒superscriptsuperscript𝑥′topsubscriptℎ𝜃subscript𝐱subscript𝐳absent𝑡p_{\theta}(X_{z_{t}}=x\mid{\mathbf{x}}_{{\mathbf{z}}_{<t}})=\frac{\exp\left({e(x)^{\top}h_{\theta}({\mathbf{x}}_{{\mathbf{z}}_{<t}})}\right)}{\sum_{x^{\prime}}\exp\left({e(x^{\prime})^{\top}h_{\theta}({\mathbf{x}}_{{\mathbf{z}}_{<t}})}\right)}, where hθ​(𝐱𝐳<t)subscriptℎ𝜃subscript𝐱subscript𝐳absent𝑡h_{\theta}({\mathbf{x}}_{{\mathbf{z}}_{<t}}) denotes the hidden representation of 𝐱𝐳<tsubscript𝐱subscript𝐳absent𝑡{\mathbf{x}}_{{\mathbf{z}}_{<t}} produced by the shared Transformer network after proper masking. Now notice that the representation hθ​(𝐱𝐳<t)subscriptℎ𝜃subscript𝐱subscript𝐳absent𝑡h_{\theta}({\mathbf{x}}_{{\mathbf{z}}_{<t}}) does not depend on which position it will predict, i.e., the value of ztsubscript𝑧𝑡z_{t}. Consequently, the same distribution is predicted regardless of the target position, which is not able to learn useful representations (see Appendix A.1 for a concrete example). To avoid this problem, we propose to re-parameterize the next-token distribution to be target position aware:
items.zh: 尽管排列语言建模目标具有理想的特性，但使用标准Transformer参数化的朴素实现可能无法工作。为了看清问题，假设我们使用标准的Softmax公式参数化下一个词分布p_θ(X_{z_t} | 𝐱_{𝐳_{<t}})，即p_θ(X_{z_t}=x | 𝐱_{𝐳_{<t}}) = exp(e(x)^⊤ h_θ(𝐱_{𝐳_{<t}})) / Σ_{x'} exp(e(x')^⊤ h_θ(𝐱_{𝐳_{<t}}))，其中h_θ(𝐱_{𝐳_{<t}})表示由共享Transformer网络在适当掩码后产生的𝐱_{𝐳_{<t}}的隐藏表示。现在注意到，表示h_θ(𝐱_{𝐳_{<t}})不依赖于它将预测哪个位置，即z_t的值。因此，无论目标位置如何，都会预测相同的分布，这无法学习有用的表示（具体示例见附录A.1）。为了避免这个问题，我们提出重新参数化下一个词分布，使其感知目标位置：

items.en: where gθ​(𝐱𝐳<t,zt)subscript𝑔𝜃subscript𝐱subscript𝐳absent𝑡subscript𝑧𝑡g_{\theta}({\mathbf{x}}_{{\mathbf{z}}_{<t}},z_{t}) denotes a new type of representations which additionally take the target position ztsubscript𝑧𝑡z_{t} as input.
items.zh: 其中g_θ(𝐱_{𝐳_{<t}}, z_t)表示一种新型表示，它额外将目标位置z_t作为输入。

items.en: Two-Stream Self-Attention While the idea of target-aware representations removes the ambiguity in target prediction, how to formulate gθ​(𝐱𝐳<t,zt)subscript𝑔𝜃subscript𝐱subscript𝐳absent𝑡subscript𝑧𝑡g_{\theta}({\mathbf{x}}_{{\mathbf{z}}_{<t}},z_{t}) remains a non-trivial problem. Among other possibilities, we propose to “stand” at the target position ztsubscript𝑧𝑡z_{t} and rely on the position ztsubscript𝑧𝑡z_{t} to gather information from the context 𝐱𝐳<tsubscript𝐱subscript𝐳absent𝑡{\mathbf{x}}_{{\mathbf{z}}_{<t}} through attention. For this parameterization to work, there are two requirements that are contradictory in a standard Transformer architecture: (1) to predict the token xztsubscript𝑥subscript𝑧𝑡x_{z_{t}}, gθ​(𝐱𝐳<t,zt)subscript𝑔𝜃subscript𝐱subscript𝐳absent𝑡subscript𝑧𝑡g_{\theta}(\mathbf{x}_{\mathbf{z}_{<t}},z_{t}) should only use the position ztsubscript𝑧𝑡z_{t} and not the content xztsubscript𝑥subscript𝑧𝑡x_{z_{t}}, otherwise the objective becomes trivial; (2) to predict the other tokens xzjsubscript𝑥subscript𝑧𝑗x_{z_{j}} with j>t𝑗𝑡j>t, gθ​(𝐱𝐳<t,zt)subscript𝑔𝜃subscript𝐱subscript𝐳absent𝑡subscript𝑧𝑡g_{\theta}(\mathbf{x}_{\mathbf{z}_{<t}},z_{t}) should also encode the content xztsubscript𝑥subscript𝑧𝑡x_{z_{t}} to provide full contextual information. To resolve such a contradiction, we propose to use two sets of hidden representations instead of one:
items.zh: 双流自注意力：虽然目标感知表示的想法消除了目标预测中的歧义，但如何构建g_θ(𝐱_{𝐳_{<t}}, z_t)仍然是一个非平凡的问题。在其他可能性中，我们提出“站在”目标位置z_t，并依赖位置z_t通过注意力从上下文𝐱_{𝐳_{<t}}收集信息。为了使这种参数化有效，标准Transformer架构中存在两个矛盾的要求：(1) 为了预测token x_{z_t}，g_θ(𝐱_{𝐳_{<t}}, z_t)应仅使用位置z_t而不使用内容x_{z_t}，否则目标变得平凡；(2) 为了预测其他token x_{z_j}（j>t），g_θ(𝐱_{𝐳_{<t}}, z_t)还应编码内容x_{z_t}以提供完整的上下文信息。为了解决这一矛盾，我们提出使用两组隐藏表示而非一组：

items.en: The content representation hθ​(𝐱𝐳≤t)subscriptℎ𝜃subscript𝐱subscript𝐳absent𝑡h_{\theta}({\mathbf{x}}_{{\mathbf{z}}_{\leq t}}), or abbreviated as hztsubscriptℎsubscript𝑧𝑡h_{z_{t}}, which serves a similar role to the standard hidden states in Transformer. This representation encodes both the context and xztsubscript𝑥subscript𝑧𝑡x_{z_{t}} itself.
items.zh: 内容表示h_θ(𝐱_{𝐳_{≤t}})，或缩写为h_{z_t，其作用类似于Transformer中的标准隐藏状态。该表示编码了上下文和x_{z_t}本身。

items.en: The query representation gθ​(𝐱𝐳<t,zt)subscript𝑔𝜃subscript𝐱subscript𝐳absent𝑡subscript𝑧𝑡g_{\theta}({\mathbf{x}}_{{\mathbf{z}}_{<t}},z_{t}), or abbreviated as gztsubscript𝑔subscript𝑧𝑡g_{z_{t}}, which only has access to the contextual information 𝐱𝐳<tsubscript𝐱subscript𝐳absent𝑡{\mathbf{x}}_{{{\mathbf{z}}}_{<t}} and the position ztsubscript𝑧𝑡z_{t}, but not the content xztsubscript𝑥subscript𝑧𝑡x_{z_{t}}, as discussed above.

items.en: Partial Prediction While the permutation language modeling objective (3) has several benefits, it is a much more challenging optimization problem due to the permutation and causes slow convergence in preliminary experiments. To reduce the optimization difficulty, we choose to only predict the last tokens in a factorization order. Formally, we split 𝐳𝐳{\mathbf{z}} into a non-target subsequence 𝐳≤csubscript𝐳absent𝑐{\mathbf{z}}_{\leq c} and a target subsequence 𝐳>csubscript𝐳absent𝑐{\mathbf{z}}_{>c}, where c𝑐c is the cutting point. The objective is to maximize the log-likelihood of the target subsequence conditioned on the non-target subsequence, i.e.,
items.zh: 部分预测：虽然排列语言建模目标(3)有几个优点，但由于排列，它是一个更具挑战性的优化问题，并在初步实验中导致收敛缓慢。为了降低优化难度，我们选择仅预测分解顺序中的最后几个token。形式上，我们将𝐳分为非目标子序列𝐳_{≤c}和目标子序列𝐳_{>c}，其中c是切分点。目标是最大化在非目标子序列条件下目标子序列的对数似然，即：

items.en: Note that 𝐳>csubscript𝐳absent𝑐{\mathbf{z}}_{>c} is chosen as the target because it possesses the longest context in the sequence given the current factorization order 𝐳𝐳{\mathbf{z}}. A hyperparameter K𝐾K is used such that about 1/K1𝐾1/K tokens are selected for predictions; i.e., |𝐳|/(|𝐳|−c)≈K𝐳𝐳𝑐𝐾\left|{{\mathbf{z}}}\right|/(\left|{{\mathbf{z}}}\right|-c)\approx K. For unselected tokens, their query representations need not be computed, which saves speed and memory.
items.zh: 注意，𝐳_{>c}被选为目标，因为它在给定当前分解顺序𝐳的情况下拥有序列中最长的上下文。使用超参数K，使得大约1/K的token被选中进行预测；即|𝐳|/(|𝐳|-c) ≈ K。对于未选中的token，无需计算其查询表示，从而节省速度和内存。

items.en: Since our objective function fits in the AR framework, we incorporate the state-of-the-art AR language model, Transformer-XL [9], into our pretraining framework, and name our method after it. We integrate two important techniques in Transformer-XL, namely the relative positional encoding scheme and the segment recurrence mechanism. We apply relative positional encodings based on the original sequence as discussed earlier, which is straightforward. Now we discuss how to integrate the recurrence mechanism into the proposed permutation setting and enable the model to reuse hidden states from previous segments. Without loss of generality, suppose we have two segments taken from a long sequence 𝐬𝐬{\mathbf{s}}; i.e., 𝐱~=𝐬1:T~𝐱subscript𝐬:1𝑇\tilde{{\mathbf{x}}}={\mathbf{s}}_{1:T} and 𝐱=𝐬T+1:2​T𝐱subscript𝐬:𝑇12𝑇{\mathbf{x}}=\mathbf{s}_{T+1:2T}. Let 𝐳~~𝐳\tilde{{\mathbf{z}}} and 𝐳𝐳{\mathbf{z}} be permutations of [1​⋯​T]delimited-[]1⋯𝑇[1\cdots T] and [T+1​⋯​2​T]delimited-[]𝑇1⋯2𝑇[T+1\cdots 2T] respectively. Then, based on the permutation 𝐳~~𝐳\tilde{{\mathbf{z}}}, we process the first segment, and then cache the obtained content representations 𝐡~(m)superscript~𝐡𝑚\tilde{{\mathbf{h}}}^{(m)} for each layer m𝑚m. Then, for the next segment 𝐱𝐱\mathbf{x}, the attention update with memory can be written as
items.zh: 由于我们的目标函数符合自回归框架，我们将最先进的自回归语言模型Transformer-XL [9]纳入我们的预训练框架，并将我们的方法命名为XLNet。我们整合了Transformer-XL中的两项重要技术，即相对位置编码方案和片段循环机制。我们基于如前所述的原始序列应用相对位置编码，这很直接。现在讨论如何将循环机制集成到所提出的排列设置中，并使模型能够重用先前片段的隐藏状态。不失一般性，假设我们从长序列𝐬中取出两个片段；即𝐱̃ = 𝐬_{1:T}和𝐱 = 𝐬_{T+1:2T}。令𝐳̃和𝐳分别是[1⋯T]和[T+1⋯2T]的排列。然后，基于排列𝐳̃，我们处理第一个片段，并缓存每层m得到的内容表示𝐡̃^{(m)}。然后，对于下一个片段𝐱，带记忆的注意力更新可以写为：

items.en: where [.,.][.,.] denotes concatenation along the sequence dimension. Notice that positional encodings only depend on the actual positions in the original sequence. Thus, the above attention update is independent of 𝐳~~𝐳\tilde{{\mathbf{z}}} once the representations 𝐡~(m)superscript~𝐡𝑚\tilde{{\mathbf{h}}}^{(m)} are obtained. This allows caching and reusing the memory without knowing the factorization order of the previous segment. In expectation, the model learns to utilize the memory over all factorization orders of the last segment. The query stream can be computed in the same way. Finally, Figure 1 (c) presents an overview of the proposed permutation language modeling with two-stream attention (see Appendix A.7 for more detailed illustration).
items.zh: 其中[.,.]表示沿序列维度的拼接。注意，位置编码仅依赖于原始序列中的实际位置。因此，一旦获得表示𝐡̃^{(m)}，上述注意力更新与𝐳̃无关。这允许缓存和重用记忆，而无需知道前一片段的分解顺序。期望上，模型学会利用上一片段所有分解顺序的记忆。查询流可以以相同方式计算。最后，图1(c)展示了所提出的带双流注意力的排列语言建模的概览（更详细的图示见附录A.7）。

items.en: Many downstream tasks have multiple input segments, e.g., a question and a context paragraph in question answering. We now discuss how we pretrain XLNet to model multiple segments in the autoregressive framework. During the pretraining phase, following BERT, we randomly sample two segments (either from the same context or not) and treat the concatenation of two segments as one sequence to perform permutation language modeling. We only reuse the memory that belongs to the same context. Specifically, the input to our model is the same as BERT: [CLS, A, SEP, B, SEP], where “SEP” and “CLS” are two special symbols and “A” and “B” are the two segments. Although we follow the two-segment data format, XLNet-Large does not use the objective of next sentence prediction [10] as it does not show consistent improvement in our ablation study (see Section 3.4).
items.zh: 许多下游任务有多个输入片段，例如问答中的问题和上下文段落。现在我们讨论如何在自回归框架下预训练XLNet以建模多个片段。在预训练阶段，遵循BERT，我们随机采样两个片段（来自同一上下文或不同上下文），并将两个片段的拼接视为一个序列来执行排列语言建模。我们仅重用属于同一上下文的记忆。具体来说，我们的模型输入与BERT相同：[CLS, A, SEP, B, SEP]，其中“SEP”和“CLS”是两个特殊符号，“A”和“B”是两个片段。尽管我们遵循两片段数据格式，XLNet-Large不使用下一句预测目标[10]，因为它在我们的消融研究中未显示一致的改进（见第3.4节）。

items.en: Relative Segment Encodings Architecturally, different from BERT that adds an absolute segment embedding to the word embedding at each position, we extend the idea of relative encodings from Transformer-XL to also encode the segments. Given a pair of positions i𝑖i and j𝑗j in the sequence, if i𝑖i and j𝑗j are from the same segment, we use a segment encoding 𝐬i​j=𝐬+subscript𝐬𝑖𝑗subscript𝐬\mathbf{s}_{ij}=\mathbf{s}_{+} or otherwise 𝐬i​j=𝐬−subscript𝐬𝑖𝑗subscript𝐬\bm{\mathbf{s}}_{ij}=\bm{\mathbf{s}}_{-}, where 𝐬+subscript𝐬\mathbf{s}_{+} and 𝐬−subscript𝐬\mathbf{s}_{-} are learnable model parameters for each attention head. In other words, we only consider whether the two positions are within the same segment, as opposed to considering which specific segments they are from. This is consistent with the core idea of relative encodings; i.e., only modeling the relationships between positions. When i𝑖i attends to j𝑗j, the segment encoding 𝐬i​jsubscript𝐬𝑖𝑗\mathbf{s}_{ij} is used to compute an attention weight ai​j=(𝐪i+𝐛)⊤​𝐬i​jsubscript𝑎𝑖𝑗superscriptsubscript𝐪𝑖𝐛topsubscript𝐬𝑖𝑗a_{ij}=(\bm{\mathbf{q}}_{i}+\bm{\mathbf{b}})^{\top}\bm{\mathbf{s}}_{ij}, where 𝐪isubscript𝐪𝑖\bm{\mathbf{q}}_{i} is the query vector as in a standard attention operation and 𝐛𝐛\bm{\mathbf{b}} is a learnable head-specific bias vector. Finally, the value ai​jsubscript𝑎𝑖𝑗a_{ij} is added to the normal attention weight. There are two benefits of using relative segment encodings. First, the inductive bias of relative encodings improves generalization [9]. Second, it opens the possibility of finetuning on tasks that have more than two input segments, which is not possible using absolute segment encodings.
items.zh: 相对片段编码：在架构上，与BERT在每个位置向词嵌入添加绝对片段嵌入不同，我们将Transformer-XL的相对编码思想扩展到也编码片段。给定序列中的一对位置i和j，如果i和j来自同一片段，我们使用片段编码𝐬_{ij} = 𝐬_+，否则𝐬_{ij} = 𝐬_-，其中𝐬_+和𝐬_-是每个注意力头的可学习模型参数。换句话说，我们只考虑两个位置是否在同一片段内，而不考虑它们来自哪个具体片段。这与相对编码的核心思想一致；即仅建模位置之间的关系。当i关注j时，片段编码𝐬_{ij}用于计算注意力权重a_{ij} = (𝐪_i + 𝐛)^⊤ 𝐬_{ij}，其中𝐪_i是标准注意力操作中的查询向量，𝐛是可学习的头特定偏置向量。最后，值a_{ij}被加到正常注意力权重上。使用相对片段编码有两个好处。首先，相对编码的归纳偏置提高了泛化能力[9]。其次，它为微调具有两个以上输入片段的任务打开了可能性，而使用绝对片段编码则无法实现。

items.en: Comparing Eq. (2) and (5), we observe that both BERT and XLNet perform partial prediction, i.e., only predicting a subset of tokens in the sequence. This is a necessary choice for BERT because if all tokens are masked, it is impossible to make any meaningful predictions. In addition, for both BERT and XLNet, partial prediction plays a role of reducing optimization difficulty by only predicting tokens with sufficient context. However, the independence assumption discussed in Section 2.1 disables BERT to model dependency between targets.
items.zh: 比较公式(2)和(5)，我们观察到BERT和XLNet都执行部分预测，即仅预测序列中的一部分token。这对BERT来说是必要的选择，因为如果所有token都被掩码，则无法进行任何有意义的预测。此外，对于BERT和XLNet，部分预测通过仅预测具有足够上下文的token来降低优化难度。然而，第2.1节讨论的独立性假设使得BERT无法对目标之间的依赖关系进行建模。

items.en: To better understand the difference, let’s consider a concrete example [New, York, is, a, city]. Suppose both BERT and XLNet select the two tokens [New, York] as the prediction targets and maximize log⁡p​(New York∣is a city)𝑝conditionalNew Yorkis a city\log p(\text{New York}\mid\text{is a city}). Also suppose that XLNet samples the factorization order [is, a, city, New, York]. In this case, BERT and XLNet respectively reduce to the following objectives:
items.zh: 为了更好地理解差异，让我们考虑一个具体示例 [New, York, is, a, city]。假设 BERT 和 XLNet 都选择两个词元 [New, York] 作为预测目标，并最大化 log p(New York | is a city)。同时假设 XLNet 采样因式分解顺序 [is, a, city, New, York]。在这种情况下，BERT 和 XLNet 分别简化为以下目标：

items.tex: \mathcal{J}_{\text{BERT}}=\log p(\text{New}\mid\text{is a city})+\log p(\text{York}\mid\text{is a city}),

items.en: Notice that XLNet is able to capture the dependency between the pair (New, York), which is omitted by BERT. Although in this example, BERT learns some dependency pairs such as (New, city) and (York, city), it is obvious that XLNet always learns more dependency pairs given the same target and contains “denser” effective training signals.
items.zh: 注意，XLNet 能够捕获 (New, York) 之间的依赖关系，而 BERT 忽略了这一点。尽管在此示例中，BERT 学习了一些依赖对，如 (New, city) 和 (York, city)，但显然 XLNet 在相同目标下总是学习更多的依赖对，并包含“更密集”的有效训练信号。

items.en: For more formal analysis and further discussion, please refer to Appendix A.5.
items.zh: 更正式的分析和进一步讨论，请参考附录 A.5。

items.en: Following BERT [10], we use the BooksCorpus [40] and English Wikipedia as part of our pretraining data, which have 13GB plain text combined. In addition, we include Giga5 (16GB text) [26], ClueWeb 2012-B (extended from [5]), and Common Crawl [6] for pretraining. We use heuristics to aggressively filter out short or low-quality articles for ClueWeb 2012-B and Common Crawl, which results in 19GB and 110GB text respectively. After tokenization with SentencePiece [17], we obtain 2.78B, 1.09B, 4.75B, 4.30B, and 19.97B subword pieces for Wikipedia, BooksCorpus, Giga5, ClueWeb, and Common Crawl respectively, which are 32.89B in total.
items.zh: 遵循 BERT [10]，我们使用 BooksCorpus [40] 和英文维基百科作为预训练数据的一部分，两者合计 13GB 纯文本。此外，我们还包括 Giga5（16GB 文本）[26]、ClueWeb 2012-B（从 [5] 扩展）和 Common Crawl [6] 进行预训练。对于 ClueWeb 2012-B 和 Common Crawl，我们使用启发式方法积极过滤掉短篇或低质量文章，分别得到 19GB 和 110GB 文本。使用 SentencePiece [17] 进行分词后，我们分别获得维基百科 2.78B、BooksCorpus 1.09B、Giga5 4.75B、ClueWeb 4.30B 和 Common Crawl 19.97B 个子词片段，总计 32.89B。

items.en: Our largest model XLNet-Large has the same architecture hyperparameters as BERT-Large, which results in a similar model size. During pretraining, we always use a full sequence length of 512. Firstly, to provide a fair comparison with BERT (section 3.2), we also trained XLNet-Large-wikibooks on BooksCorpus and Wikipedia only, where we reuse all pretraining hyper-parameters as in the original BERT. Then, we scale up the training of XLNet-Large by using all the datasets described above. Specifically, we train on 512 TPU v3 chips for 500K steps with an Adam weight decay optimizer, linear learning rate decay, and a batch size of 8192, which takes about 5.5 days. It was observed that the model still underfits the data at the end of training. Finally, we perform ablation study (section 3.4) based on the XLNet-Base-wikibooks.
items.zh: 我们最大的模型 XLNet-Large 具有与 BERT-Large 相同的架构超参数，因此模型大小相似。在预训练期间，我们始终使用完整的序列长度 512。首先，为了与 BERT（第 3.2 节）进行公平比较，我们还在仅使用 BooksCorpus 和维基百科的数据上训练了 XLNet-Large-wikibooks，其中我们重用了原始 BERT 中的所有预训练超参数。然后，我们通过使用上述所有数据集来扩展 XLNet-Large 的训练。具体来说，我们在 512 个 TPU v3 芯片上训练 500K 步，使用 Adam 权重衰减优化器、线性学习率衰减和批大小 8192，耗时约 5.5 天。观察到模型在训练结束时仍然欠拟合数据。最后，我们基于 XLNet-Base-wikibooks 进行消融研究（第 3.4 节）。

items.en: Since the recurrence mechanism is introduced, we use a bidirectional data input pipeline where each of the forward and backward directions takes half of the batch size. For training XLNet-Large, we set the partial prediction constant K𝐾K as 6 (see Section 1). Our finetuning procedure follows BERT [10] except otherwise specified333Hyperparameters for pretraining and finetuning are in Appendix A.4.. We employ an idea of span-based prediction, where we first sample a length L∈[1,⋯,5]𝐿1⋯5L\in[1,\cdots,5], and then randomly select a consecutive span of L𝐿L tokens as prediction targets within a context of (K​L)𝐾𝐿(KL) tokens.
items.zh: 由于引入了循环机制，我们使用双向数据输入管道，其中前向和后向方向各占一半的批大小。对于训练 XLNet-Large，我们将部分预测常数 K 设置为 6（见第 1 节）。我们的微调过程遵循 BERT [10]，除非另有说明³³³。我们采用基于跨度的预测思想，首先采样长度 L ∈ [1, ..., 5]，然后在 (KL) 个词元的上下文中随机选择一个连续的 L 个词元跨度作为预测目标。

items.en: We use a variety of natural language understanding datasets to evaluate the performance of our method. Detailed descriptions of the settings for all the datasets can be found in Appendix A.3.
items.zh: 我们使用多种自然语言理解数据集来评估我们方法的性能。所有数据集设置的详细描述见附录 A.3。

items.en: Here, we first compare the performance of BERT and XLNet in a fair setting to decouple the effects of using more data and the improvement from BERT to XLNet. In Table 1, we compare (1) best performance of three different variants of BERT and (2) XLNet trained with the same data and hyperparameters. As we can see, trained on the same data with an almost identical training recipe, XLNet outperforms BERT by a sizable margin on all the considered datasets.
items.zh: 在这里，我们首先在公平设置下比较 BERT 和 XLNet 的性能，以分离使用更多数据的效果以及从 BERT 到 XLNet 的改进。在表 1 中，我们比较了 (1) BERT 三种不同变体的最佳性能和 (2) 使用相同数据和超参数训练的 XLNet。如我们所见，在相同数据上使用几乎相同的训练方案，XLNet 在所有考虑的数据集上以显著优势优于 BERT。

items.en: After the initial publication of our manuscript, a few other pretrained models were released such as RoBERTa [21] and ALBERT [19]. Since ALBERT involves increasing the model hidden size from 1024 to 2048/4096 and thus substantially increases the amount of computation in terms of FLOPs, we exclude ALBERT from the following results as it is hard to lead to scientific conclusions. To obtain relatively fair comparison with RoBERTa, the experiment in this section is based on full data and reuses the hyper-parameters of RoBERTa, as described in section 3.1.
items.zh: 在我们的手稿首次发表后，其他一些预训练模型被发布，如 RoBERTa [21] 和 ALBERT [19]。由于 ALBERT 涉及将模型隐藏层大小从 1024 增加到 2048/4096，从而大幅增加了 FLOPs 方面的计算量，我们将 ALBERT 排除在以下结果之外，因为它难以得出科学结论。为了与 RoBERTa 进行相对公平的比较，本节实验基于完整数据并重用 RoBERTa 的超参数，如第 3.1 节所述。

items.en: The results are presented in Tables 2 (reading comprehension & document ranking), 3 (question answering), 4 (text classification) and 5 (natural language understanding), where XLNet generally outperforms BERT and RoBERTa. In addition, we make two more interesting observations:
items.zh: 结果展示在表 2（阅读理解与文档排序）、表 3（问答）、表 4（文本分类）和表 5（自然语言理解）中，XLNet 通常优于 BERT 和 RoBERTa。此外，我们还有两个有趣的观察：

items.en: For explicit reasoning tasks like SQuAD and RACE that involve longer context, the performance gain of XLNet is usually larger. This superiority at dealing with longer context could come from the Transformer-XL backbone in XLNet.
items.zh: 对于涉及较长上下文的显式推理任务，如 SQuAD 和 RACE，XLNet 的性能提升通常更大。这种处理较长上下文的优势可能来自 XLNet 中的 Transformer-XL 骨干网络。

items.en: For classification tasks that already have abundant supervised examples such as MNLI (>390K), Yelp (>560K) and Amazon (>3M), XLNet still lead to substantial gains.
items.zh: 对于已经拥有丰富监督示例的分类任务，如 MNLI（>390K）、Yelp（>560K）和 Amazon（>3M），XLNet 仍然带来显著提升。

items.en: We perform an ablation study to understand the importance of each design choice based on four datasets with diverse characteristics. Specifically, there are three main aspects we hope to study:
items.zh: 我们进行消融研究，以基于四个具有不同特征的数据集理解每个设计选择的重要性。具体来说，我们希望研究三个主要方面：

items.en: The effectiveness of the permutation language modeling objective alone, especially compared to the denoising auto-encoding objective used by BERT.
items.zh: 仅排列语言建模目标的有效性，特别是与 BERT 使用的去噪自编码目标相比。

items.en: The importance of using Transformer-XL as the backbone neural architecture.
items.zh: 使用 Transformer-XL 作为骨干神经架构的重要性。

items.en: The necessity of some implementation details including span-based prediction, the bidirectional input pipeline, and next-sentence prediction.
items.zh: 一些实现细节的必要性，包括基于跨度的预测、双向输入管道和下一句预测。

items.en: With these purposes in mind, in Table 6, we compare 6 XLNet-Base variants with different implementation details (rows 3 - 8), the original BERT-Base model (row 1), and an additional Transformer-XL baseline trained with the denoising auto-encoding (DAE) objective used in BERT but with the bidirectional input pipeline (row 2). For fair comparison, all models are based on a 12-layer architecture with the same model hyper-parameters as BERT-Base and are trained on only Wikipedia and the BooksCorpus. All results reported are the median of 5 runs.
items.zh: 基于这些目的，在表 6 中，我们比较了 6 个具有不同实现细节的 XLNet-Base 变体（第 3-8 行）、原始 BERT-Base 模型（第 1 行）以及一个额外的 Transformer-XL 基线，该基线使用 BERT 中的去噪自编码（DAE）目标但使用双向输入管道训练（第 2 行）。为了公平比较，所有模型均基于 12 层架构，具有与 BERT-Base 相同的模型超参数，并且仅在维基百科和 BooksCorpus 上训练。所有报告的结果是 5 次运行的中位数。

items.en: Examining rows 1 - 4 of Table 6, we can see both Transformer-XL and the permutation LM clearly contribute the superior performance of XLNet over BERT. Moreover, if we remove the memory caching mechanism (row 5), the performance clearly drops, especially for RACE which involves the longest context among the 4 tasks. In addition, rows 6 - 7 show that both span-based prediction and the bidirectional input pipeline play important roles in XLNet. Finally, we unexpectedly find the the next-sentence prediction objective proposed in the original BERT does not necessarily lead to an improvement in our setting. Hence, we exclude the next-sentence prediction objective from XLNet.
items.zh: 检查表 6 的第 1-4 行，我们可以看到 Transformer-XL 和排列 LM 都对 XLNet 优于 BERT 的卓越性能有贡献。此外，如果我们移除记忆缓存机制（第 5 行），性能明显下降，尤其是对于涉及最长上下文的 RACE 任务。另外，第 6-7 行显示基于跨度的预测和双向输入管道在 XLNet 中都起着重要作用。最后，我们意外地发现原始 BERT 中提出的下一句预测目标在我们的设置中并不一定带来改进。因此，我们从 XLNet 中排除了下一句预测目标。

items.en: Finally, we also perform a qualitative study of the attention patterns, which is included in Appendix A.6 due to page limit.
items.zh: 最后，我们还对注意力模式进行了定性研究，由于篇幅限制，该研究包含在附录 A.6 中。

items.en: XLNet is a generalized AR pretraining method that uses a permutation language modeling objective to combine the advantages of AR and AE methods. The neural architecture of XLNet is developed to work seamlessly with the AR objective, including integrating Transformer-XL and the careful design of the two-stream attention mechanism. XLNet achieves substantial improvement over previous pretraining objectives on various tasks.
items.zh: XLNet 是一种广义的自回归预训练方法，使用排列语言建模目标来结合自回归和自编码方法的优势。XLNet 的神经架构旨在与自回归目标无缝协作，包括集成 Transformer-XL 和精心设计的双流注意力机制。XLNet 在各种任务上相比之前的预训练目标取得了显著改进。
